name: California Mincemeat
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, heat olive oil over moderate heat. Add onions (peeled and coarsely chopped), garlic (peeled and minced), and green pepper (coarsely chopped). Cook for about 5 minutes until vegetables are limp.
2. Stir in marjoram, thyme, cumin, cinnamon, black pepper.
3. Add beef chuck and pork shoulder; fry meat, breaking up large chunks, until redness disappears. Do not brown the meat.
4. Add wine (Ruby port, sweet Madeira, or sherry); bring to a boil uncovered for 2-3 minutes until evaporated.
5. Stir in tomato sauce, olives with pimento, raisins, and remaining ingredients except almonds.
6. Bring mixture to simmer; cover and cook gently for 45 minutes.
7. Stir in almonds and continue cooking uncovered for 4-5 minutes until heated through.
8. Serve over rice, sourdough bread, or in pita pockets/taco shells. Makes 6-8 servings.
